reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know Q1-A is correct (my own puzzle). Across all prior games, B and C have never submitted a wrong answer on these simple arithmetic questions, so Q2-B and Q3-C are extremely trustworthy. D did make one early mistake on Q4 in Game 1, but since then their Q4 answers have repeatedly been confirmed as correct by multiple players, suggesting their current Q4-D is still quite likely to be right.

If I don’t volunteer this round, past games suggest it’s very likely no one else will either, leading to random elimination with a 25% chance I die—verifying a question would not reduce that risk this round; it only costs a coin and helps later. Given the strong track record of B, C, and D on nearly identical puzzles, the probability that all four current capsules are correct is plausibly higher than 75%, so my survival odds are better by volunteering now than by risking random death. Volunteering also ends the game immediately if correct, preserving all 4 of my coins and avoiding any random elimination for anyone.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: I know my own Q2-B answer is correct, and A and C have been consistently reliable on their arithmetic puzzles, but D is the only one who has ever produced an incorrect capsule (a Q4 in an earlier game), so Q4-D is still the weakest link. Volunteering now would mean trusting three single-endorsed answers, especially D’s Q4, which feels too risky. Passing or verifying both leave me with the same 1-in-4 random-elimination risk this round if no one volunteers, but verifying Q4 spends 1 coin to strengthen or correct the most suspect part of the password and makes any later volunteer attempt safer.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: I know my own Q3 answer is correct. A and B have never made a mistake across all previous games, while D is the only player with a known wrong answer in the past, so Q4-D is the weakest link. If I volunteered now, my main risk would be that D’s Q4 is wrong, which (given history) seems larger than the 1/4 random-death risk this round if nobody volunteers. Verifying Q4 costs 1 coin but doesn’t change my random-elimination chance this round and substantially reduces the chance I later die as a volunteer due to a bad Q4. Verifying is strictly better than passing because it gains information for the same survival risk.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q4-D → now Q4-D+B (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q4-D+B → now Q4-D+B+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D+B+C
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
